reviewer_question_cluster: Why is the Table 1B package publishable despite the unresolved exact-companion blocker?
bounded_answer_now: Because LIMEN's current public-sector contribution is a bounded evidence-architecture panel rather than a procurement-transparency result: the live five-row package already separates four disclosure and reconstruction states while keeping the missing exact buyer-side or public-assessment companion visible as the top blocker instead of smoothing it away.
paper_thesis_use: Reviewer memo, results-section defense note, thesis claim-boundary appendix, and rebuttal prep for Table 1B.
dashboard_hook: Table 1B blocker badge plus disclosure-asymmetry legend note.
uncertainty_or_claim_ceiling: Do not narrate the package as a denominator, transparency ranking, procurement-completeness result, or public-sector prevalence surface.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Only one genuinely different Amsterdam buyer-side/public-companion route or one page-specific Helsinki companion changes package strength more than another packaging pass.

reviewer_question_cluster: Why do Amsterdam and Helsinki both remain in the same panel?
bounded_answer_now: Because they expose two different ceiling problems that reviewers need to see together: Amsterdam shows that rich row-level register visibility and named governance fields do not by themselves close buyer-side or public-inspectability gaps, while Helsinki shows that same-buyer supplier-consistent award continuity can be public without reaching exact row-to-contract proof.
paper_thesis_use: Main-text contrast sentence, methods inset on exactness versus traceability, and thesis figure note.
dashboard_hook: Amsterdam caution badge versus Helsinki linkage badge; municipal-register contrast tooltip pair.
uncertainty_or_claim_ceiling: Do not caption Amsterdam as procurement-absent or resolved, and do not caption Helsinki as exact contract-linked or page-specific assessment-complete.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Reuse the Amsterdam-versus-Helsinki contrast bundle instead of collapsing both rows into one generic 'register transparency' sentence.

reviewer_question_cluster: Why are the UK ICO rows useful if they do not solve buyer-side traceability?
bounded_answer_now: Because they provide the package's strongest direct public-sector governance text on legal basis, remedies, DPIA, consent, and deployment timing, which is methodologically different from procurement reconstruction and helps show that document depth and buyer-side trace do not move together.
paper_thesis_use: Comparator paragraph, reviewer-response caveat, and thesis source-role discussion about governance depth versus procurement visibility.
dashboard_hook: Legal-basis-versus-buyer-trace split; school-biometrics and Serco comparator cards.
uncertainty_or_claim_ceiling: Do not convert regulator-document depth into supplier proof, procurement transparency, sector prevalence, or broad legality conclusions.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep the UK rows on maintenance status unless one clean official buyer-side procurement, supplier, contract, or public assessment companion appears.

reviewer_question_cluster: Why does ORION stay visible even though it is only a project-page row?
bounded_answer_now: Because the panel needs a disclosure-floor control that marks what an official public-body page can establish and what it cannot; ORION keeps the lower bound explicit so later prose cannot silently treat all official public-sector evidence as register-backed, procurement-linked, or regulator-document-grade.
paper_thesis_use: Methods note, appendix control row, thesis discussion of disclosure floors, and reviewer memo on why the package is bounded rather than flattened.
dashboard_hook: Low-trace floor comparator and project-page-only caution badge.
uncertainty_or_claim_ceiling: Do not present ORION as procurement-backed deployment, supplier-normalized reconstruction, legality proof, or operational-effectiveness evidence.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Upgrade only if a genuinely different official Slovenian procurement, register, technical, or assessment companion emerges; otherwise keep it as the floor row.

reviewer_question_cluster: What is the smallest next move that changes public-sector package strength instead of packaging polish?
bounded_answer_now: A threshold-changing public source move: one genuinely different static official Amsterdam procurement or public-assessment companion, or one already-documented Helsinki route yielding a page-specific contract, tender-lot, award attachment, DPIA, or impact-assessment document.
paper_thesis_use: Route-selection note for the next sf09 cycle, thesis roadmap snippet, and reviewer-response closing paragraph.
dashboard_hook: Priority ladder widget and blocker-order badge for Table 1B.
uncertainty_or_claim_ceiling: Do not spend another sf09 cycle on same-shape prose packaging unless a shared manuscript or dashboard surface has actually drifted.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer one real authority-changing route over more lane-local aggregation or rebuttal abstraction.
